section: Chapter 1: What is Democracy? Why Democracy?

questions.question: What is democracy?
questions.correct_answer: A form of government in which the rulers are elected by the people
questions.explanation: Democracy allows citizens to choose their leaders through elections and participate in governance.

questions.question: What does the word democracy mean?
questions.explanation: The word democracy comes from the Greek words 'demos' meaning people and 'kratia' meaning rule.

questions.question: Who should make the final decisions in a democracy?
questions.explanation: In a democracy, the ultimate authority rests with elected representatives who make decisions on behalf of citizens.

questions.question: What type of elections are required in a democracy?
questions.explanation: Free and fair elections ensure that citizens have real choices and can change their rulers if they wish.

questions.question: What does the principle 'one person, one vote, one value' mean?
questions.correct_answer: Each adult citizen has equal voting rights
questions.explanation: This principle ensures political equality where every vote has the same value.

questions.question: Why must democratic governments respect citizens' rights?
questions.correct_answer: To ensure freedom and equality in society
questions.explanation: Democracy requires protection of rights such as freedom of speech, expression and association.

questions.question: Name one argument against democracy.
questions.correct_answer: Democracy can lead to delays in decision-making
questions.explanation: Since many people participate in discussions and consultations, decisions may take longer.

questions.question: Name one argument in favour of democracy.
questions.correct_answer: Democracy improves the quality of decision-making
questions.explanation: Decisions are discussed and debated by many people, reducing the chances of mistakes.

questions.question: How does democracy help manage social diversity?
questions.correct_answer: By allowing different groups to express their views and resolve conflicts peacefully
questions.explanation: Democracy provides a peaceful method for resolving disagreements among diverse groups.

questions.question: What is the broader meaning of democracy?
questions.correct_answer: A way of making decisions through consultation and participation
questions.explanation: Democracy can apply to families, schools and organizations where decisions are taken collectively.

notes.title: Definition of Democracy
notes.content: Democracy is a form of government in which the rulers are elected by the people.

notes.title: Four Features of Democracy
notes.content: Major decisions by elected leaders, free and fair elections, universal adult franchise, and rule of law with protection of citizens’ rights.

notes.title: Universal Adult Franchise
notes.content: Every adult citizen has the right to vote regardless of caste, religion, gender or economic status.

notes.title: Arguments Against Democracy
notes.content: Critics argue that democracy leads to instability, delays in decisions, corruption and excessive political competition.

notes.title: Arguments for Democracy
notes.content: Democracy ensures accountability, better decision-making, peaceful conflict resolution and dignity of citizens.

facts.fact: Democracy is the most common form of government in the world today.
In a democracy, citizens can change their rulers through elections.
No democracy is perfect; it constantly evolves and improves through participation.
Democracy promotes equality and dignity for all citizens.
